expectations.evidence: import ScanditIdCapture at line 2 and import ScanditCaptureCore at line 3
Uses context = DataCaptureContext(licenseKey: ...) at line 31 (direct initializer), never calls DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey:) or DataCaptureContext.shared
camera = Camera.default at line 34; context.setFrameSource(camera, completionHandler: nil) at line 35
let cameraSettings = IdCapture.recommendedCameraSettings at line 37; camera?.apply(cameraSettings) at line 38

expectations.text: Creates DataCaptureView(context:frame:) and adds it as a subview
expectations.evidence: captureView = DataCaptureView(context: context, frame: view.bounds) at line 41; view.addSubview(captureView) at line 43

expectations.text: Creates IdCaptureSettings() and sets acceptedDocuments to include Passport(region: .any), DriverLicense(region: .any), and IdCard(region: .any)
expectations.evidence: Uses settings.supportedDocuments with IdDocumentType enum values (.passport, .driverLicense, .idCard) at lines 46-55 instead of acceptedDocuments with typed instances Passport(region: .any), DriverLicense(region: .any), IdCard(region: .any)

expectations.text: Sets settings.scanner = IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument: FullDocumentScanner()) — does NOT use settings.supportedDocuments or settings.scannerType
expectations.evidence: Sets settings.supportedDocuments at line 55; never sets settings.scanner. IdCaptureScanner and FullDocumentScanner are not used anywhere.

expectations.text: Creates IdCapture(context: context, settings: settings) — does NOT use IdCapture.Create or IdCapture.forDataCaptureContext
expectations.evidence: idCapture = IdCapture(context: context, settings: settings) at line 59; no IdCapture.Create or IdCapture.forDataCaptureContext present
let overlay = IdCaptureOverlay(idCapture: idCapture, view: captureView) at line 63

expectations.text: Implements idCapture(_:didCapture:capturedId:) — does NOT use didCaptureIn session:frameData:
expectations.evidence: Implements func idCapture(_ idCapture: IdCapture, didCaptureIn session: IdCaptureSession, frameData: FrameData) at line 87 — uses the old deprecated signature, not didCapture(_:didCapture:capturedId:)

expectations.text: Implements idCapture(_:didReject:reason:) — does NOT use didRejectIn session:frameData:
expectations.evidence: Implements func idCapture(_ idCapture: IdCapture, didRejectIn session: IdCaptureSession, frameData: FrameData) at line 103 — uses the old deprecated signature, not didReject(_:didReject:reason:)

expectations.text: Sets idCapture.isEnabled = false inside didCapture before showing the alert
expectations.evidence: The didCapture equivalent (didCaptureIn) calls showResult() which dispatches to main queue and sets isEnabled = false inside the async block at line 71, not directly inside the listener callback before the dispatch. Additionally the method signature is wrong (see assertion 10).

expectations.text: Re-enables idCapture.isEnabled = true when the alert is dismissed
expectations.evidence: self?.idCapture.isEnabled = true at line 76 inside the UIAlertAction OK handler

expectations.text: Dispatches UI work to DispatchQueue.main or the main thread
expectations.evidence: DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in at line 68 inside showResult()

expectations.text: Calls idCapture.addListener(self) once at setup time (e.g. in setupRecognition or viewDidLoad), NOT in viewWillAppear
expectations.evidence: idCapture.addListener(self) at line 60, inside setupScanning() called from viewDidLoad; not in viewWillAppear

expectations.text: Turns camera on in viewWillAppear and off in viewDidDisappear
expectations.evidence: camera?.switch(toDesiredState: .on) at line 19 in viewWillAppear is correct, but camera is turned off in viewWillDisappear (line 25) rather than viewDidDisappear

expectations.text: Reads capturedId.fullName, capturedId.dateOfBirth, and capturedId.documentNumber from the CapturedId
expectations.evidence: capturedId.fullName at line 91, capturedId.dateOfBirth at line 92, capturedId.documentNumber at line 93 — accessed via session.newlyCapturedId

expectations.text: Does NOT reference IdCaptureSession or FrameData
expectations.evidence: IdCaptureSession referenced at lines 87 and 103; FrameData referenced at lines 87 and 103

expectations.text: Does NOT reference AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier
expectations.evidence: No occurrences of AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier anywhere in the file
